attributes.description: Alerts when average Lambda invocation duration exceeds a configurable threshold over a 15-minute window.
attributes.name: [AWS Lambda OTel] High average duration

investigation_guide.blob:
## High Average Duration

### Triage Steps
1. Check whether a recent deployment correlated with the latency increase.
2. Review function logs for slow downstream calls (APIs, databases, SDK timeouts).
3. Compare average duration with the tail (`Maximum`) metric — a diverging tail suggests cold starts or outliers.
4. Profile memory allocation — under-provisioned memory increases CPU throttling and duration.
5. Verify VPC ENI cold-start impact if the function runs inside a VPC.

### Escalation
If duration breaches the function SLO for more than 15 minutes, investigate as a performance regression.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.lambda.otel-*
| WHERE Namespace == "AWS/Lambda"
    AND MetricName == "Duration"
    AND stat == "Average"
| STATS avg_duration_ms = AVG(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/Lambda/Duration`)
  BY FunctionName, cloud.region
// Latency threshold (ms): set per-function SLO
| WHERE avg_duration_ms > 3000
| EVAL function_id = CONCAT(FunctionName, "/", cloud.region)
| SORT avg_duration_ms DESC
| LIMIT 10
